pi_items.source: IEC 60062 standard color code calculation: Brown (1) + Black (0) + Red (×100) = 10 × 100 = 1000 µH. Verified against multiple electronics reference sources including basictables.com inductor color code calculator.
Unit conversion verified: 1000 µH = 1 mH. Confirmed by WebFetch from basictables.com which states '10 × 100 = 1,000 µH or 1 mH'.
IEC 60062 standard: Silver band = ±10% tolerance. Verified against multiple sources including learnabout-electronics.org and basictables.com inductor color code reference.
Standard 4-band inductor color code. Verified through WebSearch results and basictables.com WebFetch confirming Brown=1st digit, Black=2nd digit, Red=multiplier, Silver=tolerance.

Android standard path. /sdcard/ is standard writable location on Android devices. Path format matches Android conventions used in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts. Unverified as a specific design choice but plausible and consistent with Android app task design.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves decoding an inductor color code using the Electrical Calculations app (com.hsn.electricalcalculations, available on Google Play Store), which includes inductor color code calculator functionality. The 4-band color code (Brown, Black, Red, Silver) adheres to the IEC 60062 standard and yields an inductor value of 1000 µH (1 mH) with ±10% tolerance. All numerical metadata values are verified as correct against standard electronics color code references. The app is a real, published Android application that supports this feature.
